description: Strengthen confidence in the L3 canonicalization pipeline by expanding proptest generators to cover spaces, dots, Unicode, and complex JSON structures, then adding a cargo-fuzz libFuzzer target with seed corpus and CI integration.

userStories.title: Expand proptest generators — richer home/subpath, content templates, nested JSON, and array tests
userStories.description:
Extend the existing proptest suite in src/canon/levels.rs and src/canon/mod.rs to cover a much broader input space. No production code changes — test-only.

1. arb_home_path(): add usernames with dots (first.last), hyphens (brad-matic), and spaces (First Last); add longer home roots (/home/ubuntu, /Users/...).
2. arb_subpath(): add components with spaces (my project), dots (main.rs, v1.2.3), and deeper paths (up to 8 components).
3. Content templates: replace the single 'file at {path} done' template with a set: path-only, embedded mid-sentence, same path twice, two different subpaths, XML-attribute context, URL-then-path context.
4. Deeply nested JSON proptest: generate arbitrary JSON objects up to 4 levels deep where leaf strings may contain home paths; assert round-trip at level 3.
5. Array-of-strings proptest: place multiple home-path strings in a JSON array value and verify all canonicalize and restore correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: arb_home_path() generates at least: plain username, username with dot, username with hyphen, username with space, /home/<user> root, /Users/<user> root
arb_subpath() generates at least: plain component, component with space, component with dot, 5-8 component deep paths
Content template proptest uses all six templates defined in spec §3.1.3; each template produces a valid round-trip
A proptest named roundtrip_deeply_nested_json generates JSON objects up to 4 levels deep and asserts decanon(canon(line, 3)) == line
A proptest named roundtrip_array_of_strings generates a JSON object with an array-of-string value containing 1–5 home-path strings and asserts round-trip
All existing tests continue to pass unchanged
cargo test passes; cargo clippy -- -D warnings clean; cargo fmt --check clean
userStories.notes: Touch only #[cfg(test)] blocks in src/canon/levels.rs and src/canon/mod.rs. Do not modify production code or the L2 field whitelist.

userStories.title: Cargo-fuzz workspace, fuzz_roundtrip target, and seed corpus
userStories.description:
Create the fuzz/ sub-workspace and implement the fuzz_roundtrip libFuzzer target as specified in spec §3.2.1 and §3.2.2.

1. Create fuzz/Cargo.toml as a separate workspace (not merged into the root workspace) with libfuzzer-sys and arbitrary as dev-dependencies, depending on the chronicle crate via path.
2. Create fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_roundtrip.rs: interpret raw bytes as (home_path_len_byte, level_byte, rest=JSON); build a TokenRegistry with the extracted home; pick level 2 or 3; assert decanon(canon(line, level)) == line when canonicalize_line succeeds.
3. Create fuzz/corpus/fuzz_roundtrip/ with at least two seed files: one minimal JSON line using /Users/testuser/Dev/project as home, one representing a typical Claude session JSONL entry (sanitized).
4. Verify cargo +nightly fuzz build fuzz_roundtrip succeeds locally.
acceptanceCriteria: fuzz/Cargo.toml exists as a valid standalone workspace with [dependencies] chronicle = { path = '..' } and [dev-dependencies] libfuzzer-sys, arbitrary
fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_roundtrip.rs compiles with cargo +nightly fuzz build fuzz_roundtrip
The target correctly extracts home_path_len and level from raw bytes and handles truncated/empty input without panicking
Invariant checked: if canonicalize_line(input, level) returns Ok(canonical), then decanonicalize_line(canonical) must return Ok and equal input
If canonicalize_line returns Err (malformed JSON), the fuzz target returns without asserting
fuzz/corpus/fuzz_roundtrip/ contains at least 2 seed files, each a valid bytes encoding of a JSON line with a real home path
Running cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_roundtrip -- -max_total_time=10 against the seed corpus produces no crashes or panics
cargo test and cargo clippy -- -D warnings still pass at the root workspace level
userStories.notes: The fuzz/ workspace must NOT be added to the root Cargo.toml workspace members (cargo-fuzz manages its own workspace). Check that cargo deny check still passes at root after fuzz/ is added.

userStories.title: CI integration — fuzz build-only step in ci.yml and scheduled fuzz.yml workflow
userStories.description:
Add CI support for the fuzz target as specified in spec §3.2.3. The fuzz target must compile on every CI push but only run on a weekly schedule.

1. Add a 'fuzz-build' step to .github/workflows/ci.yml and .forgejo/workflows/ci.yml: install Rust nightly, run cargo +nightly fuzz build fuzz_roundtrip (build only, no run).
2. Create .github/workflows/fuzz.yml: scheduled workflow (cron: weekly, e.g. '0 2 * * 0') that installs Rust nightly and runs cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_roundtrip -- -max_total_time=60; fails the workflow on any crash or panic.
3. Create .forgejo/workflows/fuzz.yml mirroring the GitHub workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml contains a new step (or job) that runs cargo +nightly fuzz build fuzz_roundtrip using a nightly Rust toolchain
.forgejo/workflows/ci.yml mirrors the same fuzz build step
.github/workflows/fuzz.yml exists with a schedule trigger (weekly cron), installs nightly Rust, and runs the fuzz target for 60 seconds
.forgejo/workflows/fuzz.yml exists and mirrors the GitHub fuzz workflow
The fuzz build step in ci.yml does not run the fuzzer (build only) so CI remains fast
The scheduled fuzz workflow is configured to fail if the fuzzer finds a crash
cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check, cargo deny check all still pass
userStories.notes: Check existing ci.yml structure carefully before adding the nightly step — match the existing job/step style. The fuzz job can run on ubuntu-latest. For Forgejo, match the existing runner labels used in .forgejo/workflows/ci.yml.
